## WHAT THIS DATA IS

Stage A fingerprint: token-level diff analysis of raw vs. final transcripts across 9 MB depos.
It captures CHARACTER/TOKEN transforms (raw steno → final text), NOT front-matter rendering structure.
For D-2 (firm-group blank separator rendering), this data shows whitespace/newline transform patterns
in firm/appearances contexts — useful for frequency grounding, but does not directly encode
"how many blank lines between firm groups." That structural question requires SxS oracle comparison
across all 6 depos (see: review/*_front_sxs.txt in mrx_engine_v1).

=== KEY SIGNALS FOR D-2 (FIRM-GROUP SEPARATOR) ===
  IMPORTANT CAVEAT: Stage A fingerprint = token-level raw->final transforms.
  It does NOT directly encode "blank lines between firm groups" rendering structure.
  The separator behavior visible in the SxS oracle comparison files is a RENDERING
  decision, not a raw->final text transform. The fingerprint data cannot directly
  answer "how many blank lines between firm groups" — that requires cross-depo
  oracle comparison (review/*_front_sxs.txt).

  What the fingerprint DOES confirm:
  - Tab-to-space normalization is universal (9/9 depos): '\t' -> '    ' (4-space)
  - Newline insertion is the single highest-frequency transform: null->'\n' 29,688 occ / 9 depos
  - Newline removal also common: '\n'->null 4,563 occ / 9 depos
  - Space-to-newline conversion in firm name contexts: ' '->'\n' 3,381 occ / 9 depos
    (e.g., "PEACOCK ," ' '->'\n' "III , ESQ ." — name line breaks)
  - Double-hyphen '--' universally absent from finals: 2,048 removals / 9/9 depos

  For D-2 oracle comparison data, read:
    review/032025olsen_front_sxs.txt       (appearances pages 4+)
    review/0525black_bp_front_sxs.txt      (appearances pages 5+)
    review/060122williams_front_sxs.txt    (appearances pages 4+)
    review/082222butler_front_sxs.txt      (appearances page 3+)
    review/101322blanks_front_sxs.txt      (appearances page 3+)
    review/halprin_front_sxs.txt           (appearances pages 5+)
